name: Brown Stock
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch the bones by placing them on a bed of Mirepoix in a roast pan.
2. Add all ingredients except water to the roast pan and place in a 350 degree F oven. Roast until well browned, basting with red wine. Do not let anything burn.
3. Bring to a boil, then simmer for up to 18 hours, skimming to remove any scum that rises.
4. Scald clean containers, strain the stock into each, and add some fat from the stock to act as a protective cap. Cover with Saran wrap and cool.
5. The stock will keep at least a week but should be part of the regular weekly Mise en place. Excess can be frozen or reduced for glac d'viand, a 10-1 reduction. The fat from the top is excellent for making brown roux, as it has just the right color and flavor.
NOTES:
These recipes are for 5 gallons, but you can reduce them in half. It hardly makes sense to make less than 10 quarts, but if space is a factor, boil the stock until it reaches about half its original volume. The flavors will be more pronounced, as will any errors introduced.
